global.title: Initial-state transfer for a new participant reuses the existing document-share bundle path
global.content:
## Initial-state transfer pattern

A participant who accepts an invitation needs the current state, not just future ops. Rather than inventing a new snapshot protocol, the design **reuses the existing document-share bundle**:

- The invitation message carries a `shareReference` plus `actHistoryAddress` pointing to a one-shot drive bundle built via `buildDriveShareBundle`.
- On accept, the recipient imports the bundle. Drive, folders, and docs materialize locally with the original signatures preserved.
- After import, the recipient's SwarmChannel registers the peer feeds and starts pulling incremental ops.

This keeps the **snapshot path and the incremental-ops path decoupled**. The snapshot path is the same battle-tested import flow used by one-off document sharing; collab adds only the peer-feed subscription on top.

Because `action.context.signer.signatures` is preserved through imports (per the 2026-04-17 fix), the imported history already shows correct per-author attribution. New ops arriving through peer feeds continue the same signed chain.
global.description: A new participant joining a collab gets initial state through the existing document-share bundle — invitation carries shareReference + actHistoryAddress.
